Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Adding hamza at start makes the action happen to someone else

Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. قَدْ is a particle that emphasizes certainty or completion of actions.

Changed to the proper name Allah by adding shadda on lam. This is a unique word that refers specifically to The One True God in Islam.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being called upon)

steps.m: woe to you (said by a son to his parents)
steps.n: Added كَ to show who the warning is directed to. In this ayah, it's being said disrespectfully by a son to his parents when they ask him to believe.

steps.m: to be safe, secure, or trust
steps.n: Connected the root letters that mean safety and trust

This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. Here it emphasizes that 'Allah's promise' (وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ) is definitely true.

Changed from action to noun by adding vowel marks. Now means the actual promise itself rather than the act of promising.
Added fatha at the end to show this promise is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's specifically referring to Allah's promise.
This is the basic root that gives us the idea of worship and divinity
The root letters changed to form Allah's name, with ال and shadda showing this is The One True God

steps.m: this (referring to what was just mentioned)

steps.n: Adding the long alif (آ) at the end. In Quranic writing, this is a stylistic variation of regular alif (ا). In this ayah, it's used to respond to the question 'what is this' with 'except/nothing but tales of the ancients'.

steps.m: you all were (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state/place/time)

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something new

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a completed action. This word describes what Allah did, as mentioned in أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ (that Allah).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
The verb 'to rise up' became the noun 'sky' - things that are high up in Arabic often relate to sky/heaven
Made the word plural by adding وات at the end
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used after a preposition or as a possessive
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific, and removed the ending -i sound
Changed ending to show this is the object being created in the phrase 'who created the heavens'
The root letters أ-ر-ض become the noun 'earth/land' when we add the fatha mark (َ) on top of أ
Added accusative case ending to show this is what was created (object of خَلَقَ)
Added definite article to make the noun definite
Added conjunction to connect with previous phrase
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to negative past tense
Added conjunction وَ at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to become tired/unable to do something

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he got tired'
Added يَ at the start to make it mean 'he becomes' instead of 'he became'. The middle and end of the word changed shape to make it easier to say.
The ي is written as ى in the Uthmani script. This is just a writing change, not a meaning change.

steps.m: to create, make, or form something new

steps.n: Changes the action 'to create' into the noun 'creation' or the process of creating
Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how something is done. This connects to the word قٰدِرٍ (capable) later in the verse.

steps.m: by their creation (referring to السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ - the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added هِنَّ at the end to show this belongs to them (the heavens and earth mentioned earlier)

steps.m: to determine, measure, have power over

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who has power/is capable (describing Allah)
steps.n: Changed pattern to show who does the action. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this Quranic spelling.
Added بِ meaning 'with/by' and tanwin kasratain (ٍ) showing indefiniteness. This describes how Allah is not weary with creating.
Basic preposition showing something is above or has authority over something else
Added small alif (ٰ) to show the long 'aa' sound at the end
Added vertical fatḥa (ٓ) to show connection to the word قٰدِرٍ (capable) that follows later in the ayah
This is a connecting word that links بِقٰدِرٍ (capable) to what follows. It helps show what Allah is capable of doing - bringing the dead back to life.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added يُ at start to make it mean 'he causes/makes' instead of just 'he' + doubled the ي at the end to show ongoing action

steps.m: he gives life (acted on by قادر)
steps.n: The word changes its ending because it comes after أن, showing it's being acted upon by قادر (able to). Think of it like 'to give life' instead of 'gives life'
Root letters م-و-ت (to die) turned into the noun مَوْت (death) by adding vowels
Added ى with alif (ىٰ) at the end to change 'death' into 'dead ones' (like changing 'sleep' to 'sleeping ones')

steps.m: the dead (object of يُحْۦِىَ - gives life to)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. This word is what is being given life to in the phrase 'gives life to the dead'

This is a particle used to strongly affirm something, especially when responding to a negative question or statement. Here it responds to 'Do they not see?' (أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟) with strong affirmation.
Added an alif maksura (ى with two dots becomes ىٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to indicate slight elongation in pronunciation
Added shadda to make it an emphatic particle instead of conditional
Added pronoun هُ (he) to show who is being referred to. This connects to قَدِيرٌ (able) in the ayah, showing who is able.
Added small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or has authority over something else

Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows what Allah has power over - He is قٰدِرٍ (capable) عَلَىٰ (over) bringing the dead back to life.
Added damma (ُ) to first letter and doubled the middle letter to show completeness
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making the phrase 'of every thing'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added the genitive case ending because this word is possessed by كُلِّ (every) in the phrase كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (every thing)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to show intense quality
Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is describing Allah in this ayah
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يُعْرَضُ (is presented)
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this with what came before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: it is being displayed (on النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: Changed to passive present tense with يُ prefix and ُ ending. This shows something is being done to the subject rather than the subject doing something. In this ayah, the disbelievers are being displayed/presented to the Fire.
We start with the basic demonstrative word 'this' and change it to make it relative
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'

steps.m: those who (referring to كَفَرُوا۟ - those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) which comes right after it.

steps.m: those who (referring to كَفَرُوا۟ - those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Adding the proper pronunciation marks and changing ا to ٱ for proper Quranic spelling

steps.m: to reject or deny truth, to be ungrateful for blessings

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This shows one person did the action in the past.
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together. The small mark ۟ above وا is just to help with pronunciation.

This is a preposition showing the relationship between what comes before it (those who disbelieved being presented) and what comes after it (the Fire). In this ayah, it shows that the disbelievers are being presented upon/to the Fire.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'fire' - something that gives light and heat
Added emphasis to the first letter
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added kasra because this word comes after عَلَى (upon/on)
This is a special verb used for negation
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question. In this ayah, it's asking about هٰذَا (this) which comes right after it.
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts many demonstrative pronouns

steps.m: this (pointing to something in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ذا which makes it point to something specific and nearby. The alif at the end makes it masculine. In this ayah, it's pointing to what was just mentioned about being shown the Fire.

steps.m: this (pointing to something in the ayah)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added ال to make it specific - 'the truth' instead of just 'truth'
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with'. The alif in ال changes to ٱ after بِ
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle).
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)
This is a special word used to strongly affirm something after a negative question. Here it responds to أَلَيْسَ (is this not?) with 'Yes indeed, it is!'
Added an alif that shows the word ends with a long 'aa' sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.

steps.m: to nurture, raise, take care of
lord (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning the one who does this action completely
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something
Added نَا at the end to show this belongs to us
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before
Added fathas to make past tense form meaning 'he said'
The و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning.
Made into basic command form by adding damma on first letter and sukun on last letter
Added وا at the end to make it a command to many people instead of just one person
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result of what came before - they denied the truth, so now they must taste the punishment

Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the action. This word is the direct object (what is being tasted/experienced) of the command ذوقوا (taste/experience).
Added ٱلْ (the) at the start and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action
This is a basic preposition that shows cause or means
Added kasra to help with pronunciation when connecting to next word

steps.m: because of what (connects to تَكْفُرُونَ - you used to disbelieve)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to create a compound that connects the reason (disbelief) with its consequence (punishment)
This root represents the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Changed the root letters to past tense by adding fathas and an alif
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command meaning 'be!' by dropping the last letter and changing vowels
Added تُ to show past tense for 'I'
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by replacing تُ with تُمْ at the end

steps.m: to reject, deny, or be ungrateful

steps.n: Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowel pattern to show ongoing action
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to 'be patient!' by adding i- at the start and changing the vowels - this makes it a command

steps.m: so be patient! (connected to كَمَا صَبَرَ that follows)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect it with what follows, showing this patience should be like the patience of those mentioned next. The alif changes to a special form (ٱ) because it comes after فَ
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity
Adding مَا makes the comparison more specific and connects it to what follows. In this ayah, it connects to how the messengers were patient.

steps.m: he was patient (like the messengers mentioned after)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word relates to أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْعَزْمِ (the determined ones) who come after it in the ayah, showing how the Prophet should be patient like them.
This is a special word that comes from the concept of being first/primary, used to show possession or having qualities
Added و and alif at the end to make it plural. This word is describing the messengers (ٱلرُّسُلِ) who have strong determination.
Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun
The word is possessed by أُو۟لُوا۟ (people of), forming 'people of determination'
Adding ال makes the noun definite
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or connection
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلرُّسُلِ (the messengers). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Changed the internal vowels to make it plural (broken plural pattern)
Added 'the' to make it specific and doubled the ر with shadda
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of)
Basic negative particle that stops something from happening

steps.m: and do not (connected to فَٱصْبِرْ - be patient)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negative command with the previous positive command to be patient

steps.m: to rush or do something quickly

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to be hasty'
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or wanting to do the action
Added ت at start to change from 'he' to 'you'
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Adding emphasis with shadda shows this is definitely or surely for/to something
Adding هم at the end shows who the preposition is directed to - them
Starting with the comparison particle ك which means 'like' or 'similar to'
Adding أَنَّ changes 'like' to 'as if', making it stronger and more certain
Adding هُمْ at the end shows who is being talked about - 'they' or 'them'
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun for 'day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of seeing in the phrase 'the day they see'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and changed the last letter to ى because it's a special type of word (called weak) where ي changes to ى at the end
Changes from past tense (he saw) to present tense (he sees) by adding يَ at the start
Changed from 'he sees' to 'they see' by adding وْنَ at the end - this shows multiple people are doing the seeing

steps.m: that which (relates to يُوعَدُونَ - they are promised)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb after it (يُوعَدُونَ). In this ayah, it refers to the punishment or promise that is being discussed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show something is being done to someone rather than someone doing it
Added ون to show multiple people are being promised
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَلْبَثُوٓا۟) from present to past negative. It's like adding 'did not' before a verb in English.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added ا after وا to make the sound longer

steps.m: except (what follows is excluded from يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ - they remained)
steps.n: This is a special word that shows something is different or excluded from what came before. Here it shows they only remained for a short time, excluding all other lengths of time. It connects to the verb يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ (they remained) in the previous part.

steps.m: an hour (specific instance of time measurement)
steps.n: Root means measuring time, adding ا and ة makes it mean 'one hour' or 'a moment in time'
Added tanween to show this is the object of the verb 'they did not remain'
Basic preposition showing source or origin
The 'n' sound gets stronger (doubled) because it comes after a vowel sound when speaking
The root meaning 'bright flowing' becomes the noun for 'daytime' when we add the pattern fa'aal
Adding shadda emphasizes the word, which happens here because it follows مِن (from)
The tanween kasra shows this word is indefinite (a/any) and is being possessed by or describing ساعة (hour/time period)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added alif after second letter to create noun meaning the result of delivering
Changed regular alif to alif with overbar (purely spelling variation)
Adding tanween makes it indefinite (a message)
Basic question word used to ask yes/no questions, like adding a question mark in English
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question with what came before it, showing it's a result or consequence
Basic past tense pattern (fatha vowels) turns root letters into 'he' form of past tense verb
Changed vowels to passive voice pattern to show the action is being done to someone
Changed from past tense 'was destroyed' to present tense 'is destroyed' by adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels
This is a common exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what was mentioned before. Here it shows that they only stayed for 'an hour', excluding any longer time period.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'to stand' to noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added ال (the) at start to make it specific + ُ at end shows it's doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Made the word plural to refer to multiple people
Added 'ال' (the) at the start to make it specific, and changed the spelling style but same meaning
